questions.stem: This diagram outlines how the depicted exposure injures DNA. Under normal tissue oxygen tension, most of the DNA damage occurs through the indirect pathway. Which event is chiefly responsible for that indirect damage?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Direct ionization of the DNA backbone

choices.label: E
choices.text: Free radical formation from radiolysis of water

explanation.summary: Ionizing radiation damages DNA both directly and indirectly. The indirect effect predominates in aqueous tissue: radiation ionizes water to generate reactive free radicals (e.g., hydroxyl radicals) that attack DNA, and this damage is enhanced at high oxygen tension.
explanation.whyCorrect: Ionizing radiation damages DNA both directly and indirectly. The indirect effect predominates in aqueous tissue: radiation ionizes water to generate reactive free radicals (e.g., hydroxyl radicals) that attack DNA, and this damage is enhanced at high oxygen tension.
